_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Space occupying lesion/ tumor/ foreign body/ abscess in the orbit
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Space occupying lesion/ tumor/ foreign body/ abscess in the orbit.
Orbital tumors can be benign or malignant and arise primarily within the orbit or secondarily from an adjacent source, such as the eyelid, paranasal sinus, or intracranial compartment.
Orbital tumors can also be metastatic from distant sites.

preauth.label: CT scan of Head (including affected eye)
Clinical Photograph of the affected eye

claim.label: Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, time and date (optional)
Still image of the gross specimen removed

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes with history (of trauma, associated medical condition, etc.), clinical symptoms, examination and investigations (CT scan/ MRI brain & orbit/ USG B-scan/ blood investigations)?
Clinical photo of Affected Eye whether R or L with full face photograph?
Did the patient present with sign and symptoms of Orbit tumors confirmed on MRI (Brain + Orbit) / B-scan?
If the answer to 3.2a. is No, then did the patient present with signs and symptoms of Trauma to the eye with confirmation of a foreign body/ fracture to the orbit on MRI (Brain + Orbit)/ B-scan?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Is the document evidence present for the specimen being send for the Histopathology/ microbiology examination?

common_queries: Diagnostic imaging (MRI Brain + Orbit / B-scan) confirming Orbit tumors or foreign body/fracture to the orbit is missing or inconclusive, or clinical signs/symptoms are not documented.
Clinical notes, admission notes, or CT scan of Head (including affected eye) are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Clinical photograph of the affected eye is missing or does not include full-face view or R/L labelling.
Histopathology report for the removed specimen is missing at claim submission.
Operative notes are incomplete regarding surgical steps performed, or outcomes of surgery, or post-operative care and follow-up advice.
Still image of the gross specimen removed is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Orbitotomy Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
Orbitotomy is a surgical incision made into the orbit to allow the removal of a tumour or foreign body, to treat a lesion, or to drain an abscess.

The size, location, and consistency of an orbital mass is used to decide the surgical approach chosen. Various approaches include lateral orbitotomy, medial orbitotomy, combination lateral-medial orbitotomy, simple anterior orbitotomy and transfrontal intracranial approach.
